modules.title: DBMS Concepts and Architecture
topics: Database approach versus traditional file‑accessing approach
Entity‑relationship (ER) data model: entities and attributes, entity types
Concepts of generalization, aggregation and specialization
Transforming ER diagram into relational tables
Other data models: object‑oriented, network, and relational data models
Comparison among object‑oriented, network, and relational models

modules.title: Relational Data Models
topics: Domains, tuples, attributes, relations and characteristics of relations
Keys and key attributes of a relation
Relational query languages: SQL DDL and DML
Relational algebra operations: select, project, join, division, outer union

modules.title: Database Design and Query Optimization
topics: Normalization: introduction, normal forms, functional dependency
Decomposition, dependency preservation and lossless join
Issues with null‑valued and dangling tuples
Query optimization: introduction and optimization steps
Algorithms for implementing select, project, and join operations

modules.title: Transaction Processing and Distributed Databases
topics: Transaction processing concepts and transaction system
Testing of serializability, conflict‑serializable and view‑serializable schedules
Recoverability and recovery from transaction failures
Concurrency control techniques: locking, timestamp protocols, validation‑based protocols, multiple granularity locking
Multiversion concurrency control and recovery with concurrent transactions
Object technology and DBMS: comparative study of OODBMS vs. DBMS
Temporal, deductive, multimedia, web and mobile databases

modules.title: Relational DBMS Study and NoSQL
topics: Study of RDBMS through Oracle/PL‑SQL/MySQL: architecture, physical files, memory structures, background processes
Concepts of tablespaces, segments, extents and blocks
Distributed database features: database links and snapshots
Data dictionary and dynamic performance views
Security, role management, privilege management, profiles, invoker‑defined security model
SQL queries: data extraction from single and multiple tables, equi‑join, non‑equi‑join, self‑join, outer join
Special operators: LIKE, ANY, ALL, EXISTS, IN
Hierarchical queries, inline queries, flashback queries
ANSI SQL: anonymous blocks, nested blocks, branching and looping constructs
Cursor management: nested and parameterized cursors
PL/SQL exception handling, stored procedures with IN/OUT/IN‑OUT parameters
Triggers, mutating table errors, INSTEAD‑OF triggers

experiments: Delete duplicate row from the table.
Display the alternate row from table.
Delete alternate row from table.
Update multiple rows using a single update statement.
Find the third highest paid and third lowest paid salary.
Display the 3rd, 4th, 9th rows from table.
Display the ename, which starts with j, k, l or m.
Show all employees who were hired in the first half of the month.
Display three records in the first row, two records in the second row and one record in the third row in a single SQL statement.
Write SQL statements for rollback, commit and save points.
Write PL/SQL for select, insert, update and delete statements.
Write a PL/SQL block to delete a record; if delete operation is successful return 1 else return 0.
Display name and hire date of all employees using cursors.
Display details of first five highly paid employees using cursors.
Write a database trigger which fires if an insert, update, or delete is attempted after 7 o’clock.
Write a database trigger that acts like a primary key and prevents duplicate values.
Create a database trigger that performs the action of ON DELETE CASCADE.
Write a database trigger that should not delete from EMP table if the day is Sunday.

Rob, “Data Base System: Design Implementation & Management”, Cengage Learning

Oracle 9i Database Administration Fundamental‑I, Volume I, Oracle Press, TMH.
Paneerselvam, “DataBase Management System”, PHI Learning
dev.mysql.com, www.postgressql.org

modules.title: Introduction to Software Engineering
topics: SDLC Models: Waterfall, V-Model, Prototype, Incremental, Evolutionary, RAD, Spiral
Project Planning and Metrics for Project Size Estimation
Software Process Models and Evolutionary Process Models
Software Process customization and improvement, CMM
Analysis Modeling for Function‑oriented and Object‑oriented development

modules.title: Software Maintenance & Software Project Measurement
topics: Software effort, schedule, and cost estimation

modules.title: Fundamentals of Agile Methodology
topics: Introduction to Agile software development methodology
Comparison of Agile vs Traditional (Waterfall) development

modules.title: Software Development using Agile Methodology
topics: Applying an Agile Mindset to a Project
Agile Frameworks: Scrum, Kanban, Crystal, XP, ASD, DSDM

Rajib Mall, “Fundamentals of Software Engineering”, Second Edition, PHI Learning
James Shore and Shane Warden, “The Art of Agile Development”, 1st Edition, O'Reilly

modules.title: Introduction to Data Science
topics: Types of Data: structured and unstructured data
Data Science Road Map: Frame the Problem; Understand the Data; Data Wrangling; Exploratory Analysis; Extract Features; Model and Deploy Code
Graphical Summaries of Data: Pie Chart, Bar Graph, Pareto Chart, Histogram
Measures of central tendency of Quantitative Data: Mean, Median, Mode
Measures of Variability of Quantitative Data: Range, Standard Deviation and Variance
Probability: Introduction to Probability, Conditional Probability

modules.title: Unstructured Data Analytics
topics: Unstructured Data Analytics: Descriptive, diagnostic, predictive and prescriptive data analytics based on case study
Data Visualization: boxplots, histograms, scatterplots, features map visualization, t-SNE
Overview of Advance Excel: Introduction, Data validation, Introduction to charts, pivot table, Scenario manager, Protecting data, Excel macro basics, Introduction to macros

modules.title: Statistical & Probabilistic analysis
topics: Shrinkage Methods, Lasso Regression, Bayesian statistics
Powerful Data Analysis techniques: SUMIFS, SUMPRODUCT, VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP, INDEX + MATCH, handling formula errors, dynamic array formulas, circular references, formula auditing, pivoting

modules.title: Data Manipulation With Pandas
topics: Introduction to Pandas: understanding DataFrame, handling missing values, data operations, string manipulation, regular expressions, data cleaning, outlier detection
Visualization tools in Python: representation of pie chart, bar chart, histogram, scatterplots
Data analysis performance metrics: ROC curve, types of errors, overfitting & underfitting, model evaluation techniques (holdout, random sampling, cross‑validation, bootstrap)
Ensemble methods: bagging & boosting, gradient boosting, random forests, committee machines

modules.title: Introduction to Business Intelligence
topics: Introduction to Business Intelligence, types of Business Intelligence, modern Business Intelligence tools
Data Science and Ethical Issues: unfair discrimination, reinforcing human biases, lack of transparency
Discussions on privacy, security, ethics, and the role of next‑generation data scientists

reference_books: The Data Science Workshop, Anthony So, Thomas V. Joseph, Robert Thas John, Andrew Worsley, and Dr. Samuel Asare, Packt Publication
Python Data Science Handbook, Jake VanderPlas, OREILLY
The Data Science HandBook, Wiley Publication.
Principles of Data Science, Packt Publication.
Microsoft Excel 2019: Data Analysis & Business Modelling, L. Winston Wayne, PHI
Data Collection: Methods, Ethical Issues & Future Directions (Government Procedures and Operations: Ethical Issues in the 21st Century), by Susan Elswick, Nova Science Publishers Inc

modules.title: Introduction to Operating Systems
topics: Desirable Characteristics and features of an O/S
Operating Systems Services: Types of Services
Different ways of providing these Services – Utility Programs

modules.title: Process Management
topics: Concept of Threads: User level & Kernel level Threads
Inter Process Communication: Real and Virtual Concurrency
Solution to Critical Section Problem: Semaphores and their Operations and their implementation

modules.title: Memory Management
topics: Techniques for supporting the execution of large programs: Overlay

modules.title: File Systems Management
topics: User’s and System Programmer’s view of File System
Different Modules of a File System

modules.title: Input / Output Management
topics: Introduction to Network, Distributed and Multiprocessor Operating Systems

experiments: Write a program to implement FCFS CPU scheduling algorithm.
Write a program to implement SJF CPU scheduling algorithm.
Write a program to implement Priority CPU Scheduling algorithm.
Write a program to implement Round Robin CPU scheduling algorithm.
Write a program to compare various CPU Scheduling Algorithms over different Scheduling Criteria.
Write a program to implement classical inter process communication problem (producer consumer).
Write a program to implement classical inter process communication problem (Reader Writers).
Write a program to implement classical inter process communication Problem (Dining_Philosophers).
Write a program to implement & Compare various page replacement algorithms.
Write a program to implement & Compare various Disk & Drum scheduling Algorithms
Write a program to implement Banker’s algorithms.
Write a program to implement Remote Procedure Call (RPC).
Write a Devices Drivers for any Device or peripheral.
